items.question_text: For how long must IFR traffic be restricted or suspended following the applicable trigger time for an overdue or unreported aircraft?

choices.text: For 30 minutes following the applicable overdue-or-unreported-aircraft trigger time.
For 15 minutes, the same as a standard departure release window.
For one hour, the same duration as the ALNOT active period.
Until the aircraft is located, regardless of how long that takes.

items.explanation: IFR traffic that could be affected by an overdue or unreported aircraft must be restricted or suspended for 30 minutes following the applicable overdue-or-unreported-aircraft trigger time.

items.question_text: Under 10-4-2, for how long must airport lighting systems be operated for an unreported aircraft at a nontower or non-FSS location?

choices.text: From at least 30 minutes before the ETA until the aircraft is located or for 30 minutes after fuel is estimated exhausted.
Only during the exact time the aircraft is estimated to be overhead.
For exactly 60 minutes starting from the time the ALNOT is issued.
Only if the pilot specifically requests lights on a designated frequency.

items.explanation: Per 10-4-2, en route (nontower/non-FSS) facilities request lighting for at least 30 minutes before the ETA of the unreported aircraft until located or for 30 minutes after fuel exhaustion is estimated. Terminal facilities directly operate the lights for the same period.

items.question_text: After the 30-minute traffic suspension period expires for an overdue or unreported aircraft, what is required to resume normal air traffic control?

choices.text: The operators or pilots of other aircraft must concur, and this concurrence must be maintained for 30 minutes after the suspension period expires.
No additional requirement — traffic resumes automatically once the 30-minute suspension period ends.
Only the ARTCC watch supervisor may authorize resumption, regardless of pilot concurrence.
Traffic may resume only after the missing aircraft has been physically located and confirmed safe.

items.explanation: Per 10-4-3, after the 30-minute traffic suspension period expires, normal air traffic control resumes if the operators or pilots of other aircraft concur, and this concurrence must be maintained for 30 minutes after the suspension period expires.

items.question_text: Under 10-4-4, after how many minutes without reestablished radio communications should the aircraft's activity be considered suspicious and reported to the OS/CIC?

choices.text: After 30 minutes, matching the overdue-aircraft threshold.
Immediately upon loss of communications in every case.
Only after the aircraft crosses a reporting point without contact — there is no fixed time.

items.explanation: Per 10-4-4e, if radio communications have not been reestablished with the aircraft after 5 minutes, consider the aircraft's or pilot's activity to be suspicious and report it to the OS/CIC per FAA Order JO 7610.4, Chapter 7, and paragraph 2-1-26f.
